gloss.en: not typical of someone or something

words.example: It was uncharacteristic of her to be late.

gloss.en: in a way that is hard to see or understand

words.example: He spoke unclearly and no one understood him.

gloss.en: in a way that causes discomfort or unease

words.example: The room was uncomfortably hot.

gloss.en: not happening or seen very often; rare

words.example: Snow is uncommon in this region.

gloss.en: not worried or interested

words.example: He seemed unconcerned about the bad news.

gloss.en: not awake; unable to use your senses

words.example: They found him lying unconscious on the floor.

gloss.en: to think something is smaller or less than it is

words.example: Never underestimate the power of teamwork.

gloss.en: to experience something, especially a change

words.example: She will undergo surgery next week.

gloss.en: under the surface of the ground

words.example: They built an underground tunnel.

gloss.en: under or below something

words.example: The cat is hiding underneath the table.

gloss.en: existing or happening below the sea

words.example: They explored an undersea cave.

gloss.en: easy to understand; reasonable
gloss.zh: 可以理解的；合情合理的

words.example: Her anger is understandable.

gloss.en: knowledge about a subject or situation

words.example: Students will gain a broad understanding of the subject.

gloss.en: to start doing and take responsibility for something

words.example: They agreed to undertake the project.

gloss.en: clothes worn under other clothes, next to the skin

words.example: She bought some new underwear.

gloss.en: not imagined or thought possible before
gloss.zh: 梦想不到的；未曾想象的

words.example: They reached undreamed of heights of success.

gloss.en: having taken off your clothes
gloss.zh: 脱了衣服的；未穿衣服的

words.example: He got undressed and went to bed.

gloss.en: a feeling of worry or discomfort

words.example: There was a sense of unease in the room.

gloss.en: in a worried or uncomfortable way

words.example: She shifted uneasily in her seat.

gloss.en: not showing interest or excitement

words.example: He gave an unenthusiastic reply.

gloss.en: not level, smooth, or equal

words.example: The road was rough and uneven.

gloss.en: surprising because not expected

words.example: Things took an unexpected turn.

gloss.en: not known or recognized

words.example: The city felt unfamiliar to her.

gloss.en: not popular or in style

words.example: Those shoes are now unfashionable.

gloss.en: to open or spread out something folded

words.example: She began to unfold the map.

gloss.en: not expected or predicted

words.example: The trip was delayed by unforeseen problems.

gloss.en: having or caused by bad luck

words.example: It was an unfortunate accident.

gloss.en: not respecting religion; very unreasonable

words.example: He called me at an ungodly hour.

gloss.en: the state of being sad or not happy

words.example: Her unhappiness was clear to everyone.

gloss.en: not giving useful help or support

words.example: The staff were rude and unhelpful.

gloss.en: impossible to imagine or believe

words.example: They suffered unimaginable pain.

gloss.en: to a degree impossible to imagine

words.example: The universe is unimaginably large.

gloss.en: being the only one of its kind

words.example: Everyone's fingerprints are unique.

gloss.en: to join together to act as a group

words.example: The parties united to oppose the plan.

gloss.en: joined together by shared aims

words.example: The team was united by a common goal.

gloss.en: the state of being joined or in agreement

words.example: The leader called for national unity.

gloss.en: involving everyone in the world or a group

words.example: Education should be a universal right.

gloss.en: all of space and everything in it

words.example: Could there be life elsewhere in the universe?

gloss.en: in an unfair way

words.example: He was unjustly punished for the crime.

gloss.en: not nice or kind to others

words.example: It was unkind of you to laugh at him.

gloss.en: not known or identified

words.example: The cause of the fire is still unknown.

gloss.en: having no limit; as much as you want

words.example: The plan offers unlimited data.

gloss.en: to remove things from a vehicle or container

words.example: They began to unload the truck.

gloss.en: to open something using a key

words.example: She used her key to unlock the door.

gloss.en: clear and impossible to confuse

words.example: His voice is unmistakable.

gloss.en: in a clear, unmistakable way

words.example: The signature was unmistakably hers.

gloss.en: not normal or natural

words.example: Her smile looked forced and unnatural.

gloss.en: not formally approved or announced

words.example: These are unofficial results.

gloss.en: not liked by many people

words.example: The new tax is very unpopular.

gloss.en: not sensible or based on real facts

words.example: His goals were unrealistic.

gloss.en: not fair or sensible

words.example: It is unreasonable to expect that.

gloss.en: that cannot be trusted or depended on

words.example: His old car is very unreliable.

gloss.en: a situation of trouble or anger among people

words.example: There was political unrest in the country.

gloss.en: dangerous; not safe

words.example: The bridge is unsafe to cross.

gloss.en: not good enough; not acceptable

words.example: The service was slow and unsatisfactory.

gloss.en: caring about others, not just yourself

words.example: She is kind and unselfish.

gloss.en: impossible to stop

words.example: The team seemed unstoppable this year.

gloss.en: not achieving what was wanted

words.example: His first attempt was unsuccessful.

gloss.en: not right or appropriate for something

words.example: This film is unsuitable for children.

gloss.en: not certain or confident

words.example: I am unsure what to do next.

gloss.en: having no natural skill or ability

words.example: He is not untalented, just lazy.

gloss.en: to a degree too extreme to imagine
gloss.zh: 难以想象地；不可思议地

words.example: The task was unthinkably hard.

gloss.en: to undo a knot or something tied

words.example: He bent down to untie his shoes.

gloss.en: to (old or formal use)

words.example: He gave the gift unto the king.

gloss.en: not given medical or chemical treatment

words.example: Untreated water can be dangerous.

gloss.en: not cut to make neat or shorter

words.example: His untrimmed beard was very long.

gloss.en: the quality of being rare or strange

words.example: The unusualness of the case surprised everyone.

gloss.en: not wanting to do something

words.example: She was unwilling to help us.

gloss.en: not sensible; foolish

words.example: It would be unwise to ignore the warning.

gloss.en: not interested in money or material things
gloss.zh: 超凡脱俗的；不谙世故的

words.example: He was a quiet, unworldly man.

gloss.en: the way a child is raised by parents

words.example: She had a strict upbringing.

gloss.en: a report giving the most recent information

words.example: They send regular updates by email.

gloss.en: located above something of the same type

words.example: He bit his upper lip.

gloss.en: in a straight vertical position

words.example: Please keep your seat upright.

gloss.en: towards a higher place or position

words.example: The smoke drifted upwards.

gloss.en: connected with a town or city

words.example: Many people move to urban areas for work.

gloss.en: to strongly advise or persuade someone

words.example: I urge you to visit this place.

gloss.en: needing to be dealt with immediately

words.example: The police made an urgent appeal.

gloss.en: in a way that needs immediate action

words.example: We urgently need more help.

gloss.en: the way words are used in a language

words.example: This is a book on English usage.

gloss.en: not useful; not doing what is needed

words.example: This pen is useless.

gloss.en: a person or thing that uses something

words.example: The app has millions of users.

gloss.en: to lead or guide someone somewhere

words.example: She ushered us into the room.

gloss.en: to use something in an effective way

words.example: We should utilise our resources well.

gloss.en: to say something or make a sound

words.example: She did not utter a single word.

gloss.en: empty; not being used or lived in

words.example: The house has been vacant for months.

gloss.en: a substance that protects against a disease

words.example: Scientists developed a new vaccine.

gloss.en: a card or person you love on Valentine's Day

words.example: He sent her a valentine.

gloss.en: legally or officially acceptable

words.example: Do you have a valid passport?

gloss.en: how much something is worth in money

words.example: The house has risen in value.

gloss.en: too much pride in your looks or abilities

words.example: His vanity made him hard to like.

gloss.en: a change in amount or level of something

words.example: There is a wide variation in prices.

gloss.en: to be different in size, shape, etc.

words.example: Prices vary from shop to shop.

gloss.en: extremely large in area or amount

words.example: They crossed a vast area of forest.

gloss.en: a place where an organized event is held

words.example: The concert venue was full.

gloss.en: using spoken words

words.example: The agreement was made verbally.

gloss.en: a form that is slightly different from others

words.example: This is the latest version of the software.

gloss.en: going straight up and down

words.example: Draw a vertical line on the page.

gloss.en: used to emphasize a particular thing

words.example: He is calling her at this very moment.

gloss.en: through a place; by means of

words.example: We flew home via Dubai.

gloss.en: to move quickly back and forth

words.example: My phone began to vibrate.

gloss.en: a fast, small back-and-forth movement

words.example: You can feel the vibration of the engine.

gloss.en: success in a game, war, or election

words.example: The team won a narrow victory.

gloss.en: a person watching television or video

words.example: The show attracted millions of viewers.

gloss.en: a way of thinking about a subject

words.example: Try looking at it from a different viewpoint.

gloss.en: strength, energy, and enthusiasm

words.example: She worked with great vigor.

gloss.en: strong, energetic, and forceful

words.example: He took some vigorous exercise.

gloss.en: in a strong and energetic way

words.example: She shook her head vigorously.

gloss.en: strength, energy, and enthusiasm

words.example: He spoke with youthful vigour.

gloss.en: a large house, often used for holidays

words.example: They rented a villa by the sea.

gloss.en: a sour liquid used to flavor food

words.example: Add some vinegar to the salad.

gloss.en: behavior meant to hurt or kill someone

words.example: The film contains a lot of violence.

gloss.en: made to appear real by computer software

words.example: They built a virtual world online.

gloss.en: almost; nearly

words.example: The work is virtually finished.

gloss.en: official permission to enter a country

words.example: She had to apply for a visa.

gloss.en: that can be seen

words.example: The house is clearly visible from the beach.

gloss.en: in a way that can be clearly seen

words.example: She was visibly upset.

gloss.en: the ability to see; the power of sight

words.example: He has good vision in both eyes.

gloss.en: connected with seeing or sight

words.example: She studies the visual arts.

gloss.en: necessary or essential for success
gloss.zh: 至关重要的；必不可少的

words.example: These vitamins are vital for health.
